items.fight_news: Prochazka holds a narrow edge in early prediction markets (-121 / +101), making this essentially a pick'em - analysts rate it the most evenly contested title fight in recent UFC history.
Jiri Prochazka put his hands in a swarm of ants just to see what their bites would feel like. This is both a good and a bad thing to do.
Prochazka's first child is due just two days after UFC 327. He trained at altitude in Mexico this camp and only confirmed the fight on return, compressed and distracted fight prep heading in.
Prochazka has hinted he may wrestle Ulberg rather than strike, an unusual gameplan shift for a fighter known purely for chaotic stand-up. Suggests his team sees real vulnerability on the ground.
Prochazka publicly called out Jean Silva this week for unsportsmanlike conduct in the cage, saying 'you have to respect the athlete.' Shows he is vocal and mentally engaged heading into fight week.
Ulberg is riding a 9-fight win streak including wins over Jan Blachowicz, Volkan Oezdemir, and Dominick Reyes. His only UFC loss came at debut, he has not lost since 2021.
Prochazka's two Pereira losses both came via TKO in round 2. He admitted he was not mentally mature in those fights, but analysts note his chin has been tested and cracked at the top level.

Ulberg trains at City Kickboxing in Auckland alongside multiple champions. Prochazka trained in Mexico at altitude this camp, different elevation and prep philosophy likely to show in cardio by the championship rounds.
This is Ulberg's first UFC title shot. If he wins, he would become the first New Zealander to hold UFC gold, adding a significant narrative weight to his corner's motivation.

Murzakanov was elevated to co-main event after flyweight champ Joshua Van pulled out injured. He had less than 2 weeks notice of the promotion, unusual spotlight pressure for a fighter rarely in the spotlight.

Costa is 4 losses in his last 6 fights and is making his light heavyweight debut here. He has been bulking and posted physique content on social media calling Murzakanov 'bald and chubby Russians,' suggesting he is mentally engaged but hasn't proven the weight class suits him.
Murzakanov is undefeated at 16-0 with 12 knockouts and has gone 6-0 in the UFC, all finishes. His last fight was a first-round TKO of Aleksandar Rakic in October 2025.
Costa's last win was a UD over Roman Kopylov at middleweight in July 2025, a full year between fights and a weight class jump makes this a significant unknown for prediction models.
Analysts note Murzakanov barely registers with casual fans despite his record. A win here over a name like Costa could be his breakout moment and a title shot callout is expected if he wins.

Costa has historically struggled with cardio in championship rounds and against wrestlers. Moving up in weight to face a pure finisher on short notice is among the riskier strategic gambles of his career.
Hokit is 8-0 with two UFC first-round finishes and two Performance of the Night bonuses. He came off NFL roster stints before committing to MMA full-time, still considered raw but explosive.
Blaydes is one of the longest-tenured UFC heavyweights, fighting since 2016, and his only losses have come against elite top-3 opposition. This is considered a massive step up for Hokit.
Blaydes' last fight was a split decision win over Rizvan Kuniev in June 2025, he has been inactive for nearly 10 months heading into this bout, longest gap in recent memory for him.

Hokit's pro wrestling-inspired trash talk persona has drawn Colby Covington and Chael Sonnen comparisons. His 28-year-old age and recent activity advantage over the 35-year-old Blaydes is noted by analysts.
Blaydes owns one of the highest takedown totals in UFC heavyweight history and if he implements his wrestling early, Hokit's untested ground defense becomes the defining variable in this fight.
Reyes was KO'd by Ulberg in September 2025, putting him on a 1-1 swing in his last two outings. This fight is a must-win at light heavyweight to stay in the top 10 picture.
Walker's last fight was a TKO win over Mingyang Zhang via leg kicks and punches in August 2025. He has shown flashes of elite potential but remains inconsistent across his UFC career at 22-9.
Prediction models lean toward Reyes based on his KO win over Krylov in April 2025 and a stronger recent run, despite the Ulberg loss. Walker is rated a moderate underdog heading in.
Both Reyes and Walker have been in the title picture before but fallen short. This fight is widely seen as a make-or-break for both to stay in the top 10 at 205 lbs.
Walker's inconsistency is well-documented but his physical tools - 6'5" reach, one-punch power, and athleticism - make him a live threat in any fight he enters, especially if he gets an early finish.
This is confirmed as Cub Swanson's retirement fight. The 42-year-old is the last remaining WEC fighter on any UFC roster, closing a 25-year lineage that started with WEC 1 in 2001.

Swanson last fought in December 2024, knocking out Billy Quarantillo in round 3 for a Performance of the Night bonus. He is 3-3 in his last six and enters as a slight underdog in early prediction markets.
Landwehr is on a 2-fight losing skid, stopped by Doo Ho Choi at UFC 310 and knocked out by Morgan Charriere at UFC Nashville. The latter fight won Fight of the Night despite his loss.
Swanson's farewell was elevated to the main card after the Van vs Taira title fight was pulled, giving his retirement fight more exposure than originally planned.
Landwehr is 37 vs Swanson's 42 - two fan-favorite brawlers on the back nine who will likely produce fireworks regardless of outcome. Crowd energy for Swanson's send-off is expected to be a factor.
Pitbull vs Pico was a matchup years in the making. The two were originally booked in Bellator before the bout fell through. Pitbull has been publicly calling out Pico since both signed with UFC.

early prediction markets heavily favor Pico. At 29 vs 38, the youth and activity gap is significant - Pico is seen as the future of the division, Pitbull as the legendary name chasing one more moment.
Pico's last fight was a flash KO loss to Lerone Murphy via spinning back elbow in August 2025, a stunning stoppage that raised questions about his durability against elite strikers.
Pitbull split his first two UFC fights before beating Dan Ige by decision at UFC 318 in July 2025. He is 2-1 in the UFC but was too hesitant in his debut loss to Yair Rodriguez per analysts.
Pitbull called Pico's one-punch KO power dangerous in a pre-fight interview but added that Pico himself 'can be sparked out easily', suggesting confidence in taking the fight to the ground.

Pitbull's Bellator legacy includes two featherweight title reigns and a win over Patricky Pitbull in a sibling showdown. He enters this fight with nothing to lose and a fanbase that has followed him for a decade.
Holland is on a 2-fight losing skid, dropped decisions to Daniel Rodriguez and Mike Malott back to back. He is trying to avoid his first ever 3-fight losing streak in the UFC.
Holland fought 5 times in 2025, going 3-2. The volume is unusually high for a UFC veteran and may indicate cumulative wear, or sharp ring sharpness depending on how you model it.
Brown is rated a narrow favorite in this bout. He is 2-2 in his last four, with his most recent fight a stoppage loss to Gabriel Bonfim in his first UFC headliner at Fight Night 264.
Brown has been nicknamed 'Welterweight Jon Jones' by Nicolas Dalby for excessive eye pokes, a complaint that has come up in multiple fight camps. Referee awareness could be a match factor.
Holland's grappling has been a weakness that gets exploited at the highest level. If Brown can drag this fight to the mat early, Holland's path to a decision win narrows significantly.
Gamrot enters ranked #8 at lightweight (25-4) and is a clear favorite over Ribovics, who is unranked but unbeaten through his UFC appearances at 15-2.
Ribovics is a 15-2 Argentine lightweight who is undefeated through his UFC appearances. This is his first fight against a ranked opponent, a significant step-up in competition.
Gamrot has faced ranked opponents in two of his last three fights, going 2-1. He's known for elite grappling and relentless pace, a tough matchup for an unranked prospect still proving himself.
If Ribovics pulls off the upset, it would immediately catapult him into the ranked lightweight picture - one of the more meaningful dark horse scenarios on the card for divisional implications.
Suarez is ranked #2 in the strawweight division and lost a title shot to Zhang Weili at UFC 312 in early 2025. She bounced back with a wrestling-heavy win over Amanda Lemos at Noche UFC.
Godinez is ranked #6 after recovering from 2024 losses to Jandiroba and Dern with wins over Julia Polastri and Jessica Andrade in 2025. She enters as a moderate underdog.
Suarez is the clear favorite and this fight is widely seen as a title eliminator - the winner likely gets next crack at Zhang Weili's strawweight belt based on current rankings.

Godinez's path to an upset likely runs through her boxing and forward pressure. Suarez's elite wrestling is the defining skill gap between the two, and Godinez must neutralize it early to stay competitive.
Padilla arrived in UFC originally as a short-notice replacement 2 years ago, upset his debut opponent, and has since gone 4-0 in the UFC with 3 finishes. He enters as a significant favorite.
Mederos is 11-1 with a 9-fight win streak and is a DWCS Class of 2023 product. He has won his first 3 UFC fights by decision and has yet to be finished as a pro.
Both Padilla and Mederos are unbeaten in the UFC - one of the rare UFC bouts where both fighters enter with a perfect promotional record. One of those streaks ends here.

Gastelum is a last-minute replacement for Kyle Daukaus who fell off the card. He publicly said he has relocated his training camp to Las Vegas and described this as the start of a 'new chapter' in his career.
Gastelum is a heavy favorite in prediction models. Luque is moving up to middleweight for the first time in his career, a weight class jump that adds uncertainty for analysts.
Gastelum's recent record is 2-5 across his last 7 fights. Despite the rough stretch, he is regarded as technically sharp and the clear pick against a Luque stepping up in weight on short notice.

Luque beat Holland by submission at UFC 316 in mid-2025 and is coming off that win. Taking this fight on short notice with a weight class jump is historically a coin-flip proposition - the variables compound.
